description: Implement the first usable vertical slice of the medical care management app: authentication, patient management, conditions, medical instructions, tasks with dependencies, prescriptions, bookings, and the patient overview.

userStories.title: Scaffold backend workspace
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a backend project skeleton so that domain modules can be implemented on a stable foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create backend directory structure aligned with docs/tecnical/implementation-plan.md
Backend starts locally with Bun + ElysiaJS
Formatting and linting commands are configured for backend code
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Scaffold frontend workspace
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a frontend project skeleton so that authenticated patient workflows can be built consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: Create frontend directory structure aligned with docs/tecnical/implementation-plan.md
Frontend starts locally with Quasar
Basic authenticated shell routing structure exists
Formatting and linting commands are configured for frontend code
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Set up PostgreSQL connection and migration tooling
userStories.description: As a developer, I want database connectivity and migrations in place so that patient-scoped domain tables can be added safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend connects to PostgreSQL using environment configuration
Migration tooling is configured and runnable locally
Shared environment variable conventions are documented
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Integrate Better Auth session flow
userStories.description: As a caregiver, I want to sign up, sign in, and keep a session so that only authenticated users can access the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Email/password sign-up route is available
Email/password sign-in route is available
Session retrieval route returns the active session
Protected backend routes reject unauthenticated requests
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add authentication screens and auth boot flow
userStories.description: As a caregiver, I want sign-in and sign-up screens so that I can enter the authenticated app shell from the browser.
acceptanceCriteria: Frontend has sign-in screen with email and password fields
Frontend has sign-up screen with full name, email, password, and confirm password fields
Authenticated users are redirected into the app shell after login
Unauthenticated users cannot access protected frontend routes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create user profile support
userStories.description: As a user, I want my profile and language preference stored so that the app can identify me and support i18n from the start.
acceptanceCriteria: Current-user endpoint returns id, full name, email, and preferred language
Preferred language supports values it and en
Frontend authenticated shell loads current-user data after session restore
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add core patient access tables
userStories.description: As a developer, I want patient and patient-user tables in the database so that all later domain data can be authorized per patient.
acceptanceCriteria: Add patients table with UUID primary key, optional date_of_birth, notes, deleted_at, created_at, and updated_at
Add patient_users table with unique patient_id and user_id pair
Migration runs successfully on a clean database
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement patient backend CRUD and authorization
userStories.description: As a caregiver, I want patient CRUD APIs so that I can manage the people whose care workflows I track.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement patient list, create, get, update, archive, and restore endpoints
Archive operation performs soft deletion by setting deleted_at
All patient routes enforce patient membership authorization server-side
Archived patients are excluded from default list responses
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Build patient list and patient form UI
userStories.description: As a caregiver, I want patient list and create/edit flows so that I can enter and access patient records from the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Patient list page shows accessible active patients
Patient form supports full name, date of birth, and notes
Patient list supports archived toggle or restore path
Creating a patient navigates to patient overview
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add condition schema and backend routes
userStories.description: As a caregiver, I want conditions stored per patient so that instructions and tasks can be organized in clinical context.
acceptanceCriteria: Add conditions table linked to patients with name, notes, active, created_at, and updated_at
Implement condition list, create, update, and deactivate or archive routes
Condition routes enforce patient-scoped authorization
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Build condition management UI
userStories.description: As a caregiver, I want to add and update conditions inside patient context so that the patient overview stays clinically organized.
acceptanceCriteria: Patient context shows active conditions list
User can add a condition with name, notes, and active state
User can edit an existing condition
Inactive or archived conditions are distinguishable from active ones
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add medical instruction schema and backend routes
userStories.description: As a caregiver, I want specialist instructions stored with original notes so that the care plan is preserved and can generate tasks.
acceptanceCriteria: Add medical_instructions table with doctor name or specialty, instruction date, original notes, target timing text, status, and created_by_user_id
Implement instruction list, create, get, and update routes
Original notes are stored exactly as entered
Instruction routes support status and date filtering
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Build instruction list and form UI
userStories.description: As a caregiver, I want to create and review medical instructions from the browser so that specialist plans are visible in patient context.
acceptanceCriteria: Instruction list shows doctor or specialty, instruction date, summary, and status
Instruction form supports doctor name, specialty, instruction date, original notes, and target timing text
Instruction detail shows original notes and linked tasks section
Instruction list supports status or date filtering
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add task schema and backend CRUD
userStories.description: As a caregiver, I want structured tasks stored per patient so that care actions can be tracked with due dates and statuses.
acceptanceCriteria: Add tasks table with patient_id, optional medical_instruction_id, optional condition_id, title, description, task_type, status, due_date, scheduled_at, auto_recurrence_enabled, recurrence_rule, completed_at, deleted_at, created_at, and updated_at
Task status supports pending, blocked, scheduled, completed, cancelled, and deferred
Implement task list, create, get, update, archive, and status change routes
Automatic recurrence is disabled by default for new tasks
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add task dependency validation
userStories.description: As a caregiver, I want task prerequisites enforced so that blocked steps and next actions are reliable.
acceptanceCriteria: Add task_dependencies table with task_id and depends_on_task_id
Create route or service support to add task dependencies
Reject circular dependencies
Reject dependencies across different patients
Blocked task evaluation uses incomplete prerequisites
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Build task list and task editor UI
userStories.description: As a caregiver, I want to create, update, and review tasks so that I can see overdue, blocked, upcoming, and completed work clearly.
acceptanceCriteria: Task list separates overdue, blocked, upcoming, and completed items
Task form supports title, description, task type, due date, optional scheduled datetime, instruction link, and condition link
Task detail or editor allows status updates
Blocked tasks are visually distinct from actionable tasks
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add prescription schema and backend workflow
userStories.description: As a caregiver, I want prescriptions tracked with explicit states so that GP-related work is visible and auditable.
acceptanceCriteria: Add prescriptions table with patient_id, optional task_id, optional medication_id, prescription_type, status, requested_at, received_at, collected_at, issue_date, expiration_date, notes, deleted_at, created_at, and updated_at
Prescription status supports needed, requested, available, collected, used, expired, and cancelled
Implement prescription list, create, get, update, and status change routes
Server-side validation distinguishes requested, available, and collected as separate states
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Build prescription tracker UI
userStories.description: As a caregiver, I want to manage prescription states in patient context so that I can see what the GP workflow still requires.
acceptanceCriteria: Prescription view lists patient prescriptions with type and status
User can create or edit a prescription with key dates and notes
User can update prescription workflow state from the UI
Prescription records can link back to the related task when present
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add booking and facility schema with backend routes
userStories.description: As a caregiver, I want bookings stored with facility context so that appointments can be tracked against tasks and prescriptions.
acceptanceCriteria: Add facilities table with name, type, location details, notes, created_at, and updated_at
Add bookings table with patient_id, task_id, optional prescription_id, optional facility_id, provider or facility fields, booking date, appointment datetime, status, notes, created_at, and updated_at
Booking status supports not_booked, booking_in_progress, booked, completed, and cancelled
Implement facility CRUD needed for booking creation
Implement booking list, create, get, update, and status change routes
Server-side validation rejects invalid booking status transitions
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Build booking management UI
userStories.description: As a caregiver, I want to create and review bookings so that upcoming exams and visits are visible in patient context.
acceptanceCriteria: Booking form supports task link, optional prescription link, facility or provider, booking date, appointment datetime, status, and notes
Booking list shows upcoming appointments clearly
User can update booking status from the UI
Facility selection or creation path is available during booking management
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add patient overview aggregation endpoint
userStories.description: As a caregiver, I want a summarized patient overview API so that the UI can present the current operational state quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create patient overview endpoint returning overdue task count, upcoming appointments, pending prescriptions, and active conditions
Overview endpoint only returns data for authorized patient members
Overview aggregates use existing patient-scoped domain data without duplicating source records
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Build patient overview UI
userStories.description: As a caregiver, I want a patient overview page so that I can understand the current care state and take the next action quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Patient overview shows overdue tasks, upcoming appointments, pending prescriptions, and active conditions
Overview provides quick actions for adding instruction, task, prescription, and booking
Overview is reachable after patient creation and when opening an existing patient
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
